problem: Vibration + visible seal leak from bottom of housing. Grinding noise reported by operator.
root_cause: Worn impeller shaft (0.004" runout) causing seal face damage and misalignment.
resolution: Replaced shaft assembly, new mechanical seal (SEAL-X450-22), both bearings. Performed precision alignment.
outcome: Resolved - unit running at 0.8 mm/s vibration after repair
technician_notes: Always check shaft runout with dial indicator before installing new seal. Customer had skipped the last two quarterly services.

problem: Grinding noise from motor end + housing temperature 92°C (normal 65°C).
root_cause: Drive end bearing failure due to insufficient lubrication and contamination.
resolution: Replaced drive end and non-drive end bearings. Cleaned housing, installed new grease fittings and performed full alignment check.
outcome: Resolved. Vibration reduced from 4.2 to 0.6 mm/s.
technician_notes: Customer admitted to skipping the last two scheduled services. Added reminder in CMMS.

problem: Sudden drop in flow rate (from 420 GPM to 280 GPM) with increased motor amps.
root_cause: Partial blockage in impeller vanes from process debris + worn impeller.
resolution: Removed pump, cleaned impeller, replaced impeller with new stainless unit. Verified NPSH available.
outcome: Flow restored to 435 GPM. No further issues.
technician_notes: Recommend installing suction strainer on this application.
